# CiCwtch - Security Policy
## Repository Standards & Collaboration

<p align="left">
  <a href="https://github.com/features/copilot"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-181717?style=for-the-badge&logo=github&logoColor=white" alt="GitHub" /></a>
  &nbsp;
  <a href="https://flutter.dev/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Flutter-02569B?style=for-the-badge&logo=flutter&logoColor=white" alt="Flutter" /></a>
  &nbsp;
  <a href="https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Cloudflare%20Workers-F38020?style=for-the-badge&logo=cloudflare&logoColor=white" alt="Cloudflare Workers" /></a>
</p>

## Supported scope

Security reports are welcomed for the active default branch and any currently maintained release branches.

## Reporting a vulnerability

Please do **not** open a public GitHub issue for a suspected security problem.

Instead:

- use the support route in `.github/SUPPORT.md`; or
- contact the maintainer privately through the project Discord server and request a private handoff.

Include, where possible:

- a clear summary of the issue;
- affected files, endpoints, or components;
- reproduction steps;
- impact assessment;
- any suggested remediation.

## Response approach

Valid reports will be reviewed, prioritised, and remediated as quickly as practical. Public disclosure should wait until a fix or mitigation is available.
